title: zieria minutiflora subsp. minutiflora
intro: Zieria minutiflora subsp. minutiflora, also known as the small-flowered zieria, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to southeastern Australia. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including pain relief, reduced inflammation, and antioxidant activity, making it a potential natural remedy for various health issues. Zieria minutiflora subsp. minutiflora can be prepared as an essential oil, tincture, or infusion, which can be used topically or internally, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
